acceptance_criteria.title: No hardcoded OLLAMA_TOKEN in systemd service file
acceptance_criteria.description: The jit-heartbeat.service file shall not contain OLLAMA_TOKEN= or any token value in plain text
validation: systemctl show jit-heartbeat.service -p Environment | grep -i token | wc -l == 0

acceptance_criteria.title: Credentials stored with proper file permissions
acceptance_criteria.description: OLLAMA_TOKEN credential file must be stored with mode 0600 (readable only by root), owned by root:root

acceptance_criteria.title: LoadCredential mechanism properly configured
acceptance_criteria.description: systemd service shall use LoadCredential= to load OLLAMA_TOKEN from /etc/jit-credentials/ollama_token

acceptance_criteria.title: Service starts and runs successfully
acceptance_criteria.description: systemd service shall start without errors and maintain active status for at least 5 minutes
validation: systemctl start jit-heartbeat && sleep 5 && systemctl is-active jit-heartbeat | grep -i 'active'
journalctl -u jit-heartbeat --since='5 minutes ago' | grep -i 'error\|failed' | wc -l == 0

acceptance_criteria.title: Token not visible in process environment
acceptance_criteria.description: Running process shall not expose OLLAMA_TOKEN in process environment (ps aux output)
validation: ps aux | grep -i heartbeat | grep -v grep | grep 'OLLAMA_TOKEN' | wc -l == 0

acceptance_criteria.title: Token removed from git history
acceptance_criteria.description: No instance of hardcoded token (9e34679) shall exist in any git commit or branch history
validation: git rev-list --all | xargs git grep '9e34679' 2>/dev/null | wc -l == 0

acceptance_criteria.title: Ollama API connectivity verified
acceptance_criteria.description: Service shall successfully connect to Ollama API endpoint using credentials from secure store
validation: journalctl -u jit-heartbeat --since='10 minutes ago' | grep -iE 'ollama|7778|api' | wc -l >= 1
journalctl -u jit-heartbeat --since='10 minutes ago' | grep -iE '401|403|unauthorized|forbidden' | wc -l == 0
journalctl -u jit-heartbeat --since='10 minutes ago' | grep -i 'error' | wc -l == 0

acceptance_criteria.title: Token rotated and old token disabled
acceptance_criteria.description: Old exposed token (9e34679) must be disabled in MDES system; new token must be active and stored securely
validation: [Manual verification]: MDES Ollama dashboard shows old token DISABLED, new token ACTIVE

acceptance_criteria.title: Setup credentials script creates valid configuration
acceptance_criteria.description: setup-credentials.sh shall correctly create credentials directory and file with proper permissions

acceptance_criteria.title: System health check passes
acceptance_criteria.description: Full multi-agent system shall remain healthy after credential migration (body-check.sh)

test_cases.title: Service file does not contain hardcoded token
test_cases.objective: Verify no OLLAMA_TOKEN environment variable exists in service file with hardcoded value
setup: Ensure /workspaces/Jit/jit-heartbeat.service is in current state post-fix

steps.action: Search for Environment= lines with OLLAMA_TOKEN
Search for old hardcoded token value

test_cases.expected_result: Steps 2-3 return no matches; Step 4 returns LoadCredential line
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-001-PASS if all grep commands return correctly (no token, LoadCredential present)
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Service file still contains hardcoded token or incorrect credential mechanism

test_cases.title: Credentials directory exists with correct permissions
test_cases.objective: Verify /etc/jit-credentials exists with mode 0700 and is owned by root:root
setup: Ensure credentials setup script has been executed with sudo
test_cases.expected_result: Directory exists with permissions 700 (drwx------) owned by root:root
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-002-PASS if stat output shows '700 root:root'
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Directory missing or has incorrect permissions

test_cases.title: Token credential file has restrictive permissions
test_cases.objective: Verify /etc/jit-credentials/ollama_token exists with mode 0600, owned by root:root, not world-readable
setup: Credentials directory must be properly set up (TC-002 passed)
test_cases.expected_result: File exists, mode 0600 (owned root:root), not world-readable, matches pattern -rw-------
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-003-PASS if stat shows '600 root:root' AND ls shows -rw------- pattern
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: File missing, wrong permissions, or world-readable

test_cases.title: Service file passes systemd syntax validation
test_cases.objective: Verify jit-heartbeat.service is valid systemd unit file without syntax errors
setup: systemd package must be installed (standard on all Linux systems)
test_cases.expected_result: systemd-analyze returns 'OK' with no errors; systemctl version >= 247; service loads without errors
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-004-PASS if systemd-analyze output contains 'OK' and no 'error' messages
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Service file has syntax errors or LoadCredential not supported

test_cases.title: Service starts and runs without errors
test_cases.objective: Verify jit-heartbeat.service successfully starts and remains in active state
setup: Credentials must be properly configured (TC-003 passed)

steps.action: Check for startup errors in logs

test_cases.expected_result: Service enters 'active (running)' state; is-active returns 'active'; no error messages in journal
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-005-PASS if 'systemctl is-active' returns 'active' AND no errors in step 6
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Service fails to start, enters failed state, or reports errors

test_cases.title: Running process does not expose token in environment
test_cases.objective: Verify OLLAMA_TOKEN is not visible when inspecting running heartbeat process

steps.action: Check process environment via ps aux
steps.command: ps aux | grep -i heartbeat | grep -v grep

steps.action: Check for token value in command line
steps.command: ps aux | grep heartbeat | grep '9e34679' || echo 'NOT_FOUND'

test_cases.expected_result: Steps 3-5 return 'NOT_FOUND'; Step 4 shows 'Environment=' without OLLAMA_TOKEN value
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-006-PASS if token is not visible in any output (steps 2-5)
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Token is visible in process environment or command line

test_cases.title: Hardcoded token removed from all git history
test_cases.objective: Verify no instance of exposed token (9e34679) exists in any commit or branch
setup: Git history scrubbing tool must have been executed (BFG or git-filter-repo)

steps.action: Search git history for token across all commits
Search git object database for token
steps.command: git rev-list --all | xargs git grep '9e34679' 2>/dev/null | head -10
for branch in $(git branch -a | sed 's|^[* ] ||'); do git log $branch -S '9e34679' --oneline 2>/dev/null | head -1; done

test_cases.expected_result: All steps return empty/no results; token completely absent from git history and working tree
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-007-PASS if steps 1-4 all return zero matches or 'NOT_FOUND'
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Token found in git history or current working tree

test_cases.title: Service successfully connects to Ollama API
test_cases.objective: Verify jit-heartbeat service can authenticate and call Ollama API using credential from secure store
setup: Ollama API must be accessible at https://ollama.mdes-innova.online
Token in /etc/jit-credentials/ollama_token must be valid and active

steps.action: Check recent service logs for Ollama API calls
steps.command: journalctl -u jit-heartbeat --since='30 minutes ago' | grep -iE 'ollama|7778|api' | head -5

steps.action: Manually test Ollama API with stored token

test_cases.expected_result: Step 1-3 show API calls without auth errors; Step 4 returns 200 OK with health status
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-008-PASS if no 401/403 errors AND step 4 returns HTTP 200 with valid JSON
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Auth failures (401/403) or Ollama API unreachable

test_cases.title: setup-credentials.sh correctly initializes credential store
test_cases.objective: Verify setup-credentials.sh creates directory, stores token, sets permissions correctly

steps.action: Check script exists and is executable

test_cases.expected_result: Script is executable, has proper usage documentation, validates input, creates directory with correct ownership/permissions
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-009-PASS if script is executable AND step 4 shows error handling for missing token
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Script not executable, missing validation, or incorrect permission setup

test_cases.title: Service restarts cleanly after failure
test_cases.objective: Verify service restart policy works correctly (Restart=always, RestartSec=10)
test_cases.expected_result: Service stops, restarts, and returns to active state with new process
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-010-PASS if step 3 shows 'inactive' AND step 6 shows 'active' with valid PID
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Service doesn't restart or enters failed state

test_cases.title: Full multi-agent system remains healthy
test_cases.objective: Verify credential migration doesn't break overall system (body-check.sh, soul-check.sh)
test_cases.expected_result: soul-check and body-check report OK/PASS; error count = 0; Oracle API responds
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-011-PASS if error count is 0 AND body-check shows 'PASS' or 'OK' overall
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Health checks fail or report errors related to credential/auth

test_cases.title: Heartbeat pulses continue at expected interval
test_cases.objective: Verify service sends heartbeat pulses to Oracle at configured interval (no skipped pulses due to auth failure)

steps.command: echo 'Waiting for heartbeat pulse (configured every 15 minutes)...'

steps.action: Check for recent heartbeat events in Oracle or logs
steps.command: journalctl -u jit-heartbeat --since='25 minutes ago' | grep -iE 'pulse|heartbeat|pran' | head -5
journalctl -u jit-heartbeat --since='25 minutes ago' | grep -iE '401|403|unauthorized' | wc -l

test_cases.expected_result: Recent heartbeat events found; no auth failures blocking pulses
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-012-PASS if step 3 shows heartbeat activity AND step 4 returns 0
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: No heartbeat pulses sent or auth failures blocking communication

test_cases.title: Non-root user cannot read token credential
test_cases.objective: Verify standard users cannot access OLLAMA_TOKEN even if running service commands
setup: Credentials must be set up (TC-003 passed)
Test user must exist or create temporary test user

steps.action: Attempt to read credential as non-root

steps.command: sudo -u testuser cat /etc/jit-credentials/ollama_token 2>&1 | grep -iE 'permission|denied|cannot' && echo 'DENIED' || echo 'READABLE'

test_cases.expected_result: Non-root user gets 'Permission denied'; root can read successfully
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-013-PASS if step 3 shows 'DENIED' AND step 4 shows token length > 0
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Non-root user can read token or root cannot read

test_cases.title: Service handles invalid/expired token gracefully
test_cases.objective: Verify service logs auth error but continues running when token is invalid (doesn't crash)
setup: Must have ability to temporarily update credentials file

steps.action: Check if service is still active (doesn't crash on invalid token)

steps.command: sudo cp /tmp/ollama_token.backup /etc/jit-credentials/ollama_token && sudo systemctl restart jit-heartbeat

test_cases.expected_result: Service stays active even with invalid token; auth errors logged; recovers when valid token restored
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-014-PASS if step 4 shows 'active' AND step 5 shows auth error (graceful failure)
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Service crashes or enters failed state on invalid token

test_cases.title: Service handles missing credentials directory gracefully
test_cases.objective: Verify service error handling when /etc/jit-credentials directory is deleted

steps.action: Check for LoadCredential error in logs

steps.command: sudo cp -r /tmp/jit-credentials.backup /etc/jit-credentials && sudo systemctl restart jit-heartbeat

test_cases.expected_result: Service fails to start or stays inactive with clear error in logs; recovers when credentials restored
test_cases.pass_criteria: TC-015-PASS if step 4 shows 'inactive' or 'failed' AND step 5 shows credential error
test_cases.failure_mode: FAIL: Service starts without credentials or no error logged

regression_tests.title: No credential leakage via systemd-show
regression_tests.description: systemctl show should not expose any credential values in Environment= output
regression_tests.command: systemctl show jit-heartbeat.service -p Environment | grep -E 'OLLAMA_TOKEN=.+' || echo 'SAFE'

regression_tests.title: No credential leakage via systemd-analyze security
regression_tests.description: systemd-analyze security should report no exposed secrets

regression_tests.title: Heartbeat pulse continues after service restart
regression_tests.description: After service restart, heartbeat should resume pulses to Oracle within configured interval
regression_tests.command: for i in 1 2 3; do systemctl restart jit-heartbeat && sleep 30 && systemctl is-active jit-heartbeat; done

regression_tests.title: Credential persistence across system reboot
regression_tests.description: After system reboot, service should start with credentials still in place
regression_tests.command: sudo systemctl reboot; sleep 60; systemctl is-active jit-heartbeat

regression_tests.title: No regressions in other services using EnvironmentFile
regression_tests.description: Other systemd services using EnvironmentFile should continue working
regression_tests.command: for svc in hermes-discord jit-heartbeat $(systemctl list-unit-files | grep jit | cut -d' ' -f1); do systemctl is-active $svc 2>/dev/null && echo "$svc: OK" || echo "$svc: FAILED"; done

regression_tests.title: Audit trail captures credential access patterns
regression_tests.description: auditd/journald should show access to /etc/jit-credentials only by authorized processes
regression_tests.expected: Entries show only root/systemd accessing credentials (no unexpected processes)

regression_tests.title: Documentation stays in sync with credential mechanism
regression_tests.description: CLAUDE.md should document current credential mechanism (LoadCredential)
regression_tests.expected: >= 1 (at least one reference to current mechanism)

regression_tests.title: Compatibility with existing heartbeat-24h-daemon.sh
regression_tests.description: heartbeat-24h-daemon.sh should read OLLAMA_TOKEN from environment (set by systemd)
regression_tests.expected: Script references OLLAMA_TOKEN from environment, not hardcoded

regression_tests.title: LoadCredential fallback works if /etc/jit-credentials unavailable
regression_tests.description: Service should fail gracefully (not crash) if LoadCredential path doesn't exist
regression_tests.expected: Shows LoadCredential path; service logs clear error if path missing

regression_tests.title: No secrets in journalctl output
regression_tests.description: Sensitive token values should never appear in systemd journal logs

performance_benchmarks.baseline: < 2 seconds from systemctl start to active state
performance_benchmarks.test_command: time systemctl restart jit-heartbeat && sleep 1 && systemctl is-active jit-heartbeat

performance_benchmarks.metric: First Ollama API call after startup
performance_benchmarks.test_command: systemctl restart jit-heartbeat && sleep 2 && journalctl -u jit-heartbeat --since='5s ago' | grep -i 'ollama' | head -1
performance_benchmarks.acceptance_criteria: API call appears in logs within 5 seconds

performance_benchmarks.test_command: ps aux | grep jit-heartbeat | grep -v grep | awk '{print $6}'
performance_benchmarks.acceptance_criteria: RSS memory < 100 MB for heartbeat process

test_reporting.approval_gate: All P0 tests must PASS before merge to main; P1 tests should PASS before production deployment
